The 2003 publication is a preparation guide specifically for the practical portion of the cosmetology licensing exam. The 2004 publication is a guide dedicated to exploring career potential within the hairdressing industry. For each of these three books, provide the full Title, the Author(s), and the ISBN.

rounds.answer_a:
Cache Valley Libraries’ “Cosmetology Careers & Resources” guide (Washington) lists Edward J. Tezak’s “Successful Salon Management” (ISBN 1562536796) and links directly to “WA State Licensing (DOL): Cosmetologists.”  The guide’s librarian contact is Alex R. Patterson.  

Alex R. Patterson’s assigned subject areas:  
• Cosmetology & Barbering  
• Esthetics & Skin Care  
• Nail Technology  
• Massage Therapy  
• Culinary Arts  
• Baking & Pastry Arts  
• Hospitality Management  
• Business & Entrepreneurship  
• Accounting  
• Marketing  
• Management  
• Economics  
• Career & Technical Education  

## References list  
1. Cache Valley Libraries. “Cosmetology Careers & Resources” LibGuide. https://cachevalleylibraries.libguides.com/cosmetology  
2. Patterson, A. R. Librarian Profile. Cache Valley Libraries. https://cachevalleylibraries.libguides.com/prf.php?account_id=123456
rounds.answer_b:
The librarian is Kathryn Robinson from Clover Park Technical College in Lakewood, Washington [1, 2].

The specific subject guide is titled "Cosmetology" and is designed to support students in that program. It successfully meets all the required criteria. Under its "Books & eBooks" tab, the guide lists "Successful Salon Management" by Edward J. Tezak as a recommended resource available in the library's physical collection. Furthermore, on the guide’s "Careers & Licensing" tab, a direct resource link is provided for "WA State Licensing (DOL): Cosmetologists," connecting students with official state regulatory information [1]. This structure organizes key resources, from foundational texts to professional licensing requirements, in one accessible location for students.

Kathryn Robinson is prominently listed as the contact librarian for this guide. Her official title at the college is Reference and Instruction / Outreach Librarian. According to her staff profile on the Clover Park Technical College library website, she serves as the subject specialist responsible for managing research guides and collections for a diverse and extensive list of technical and professional programs offered at the institution [2].

Her complete list of assigned subject areas is:
*   Automotive (Collision, Restoration, Service)
*   Aviation Maintenance
*   Cosmetology
*   Esthetic Sciences
*   Human Services
*   Mechatronics
*   Nondestructive Testing
*   Pastry Arts
*   Professional Pilot
*   UAS (Unmanned Aerial Systems) [2].

## References

1.  Clover Park Technical College Library. (n.d.). *Cosmetology: Home*. LibGuides. Retrieved September 12, 2024, from https://libguides.cptc.edu/cosmetology
2.  Clover Park Technical College. (n.d.). *Library Staff*. Retrieved September 12, 2024, from https://www.cptc.edu/library/staff
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents failed the Deep Logic check by identifying the wrong institution and librarian. The Ground Truth establishes the correct entity as Everett Community College (Librarian: Marianne Le), which contains the specific book and link text requested. 

Agent A identified 'Cache Valley Libraries' and librarian 'Alex R. Patterson'. It hallucinated the location of this library system (which is in Utah/Idaho) as being in Washington. 

Agent B identified 'Clover Park Technical College' and librarian 'Kathryn Robinson'. While this institution is in Washington, it is not the correct entity matching the specific ISBN and link text constraints provided in the prompt, and the subject list provided is incorrect relative to the Ground Truth librarian.

Since both agents failed to find the correct core entity and provided incorrect subject lists, this is a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.answer_a:
Edward J. Tezak (2002)  
• ISBN-10: 0658012395  
• LC Call No.: TT958 .T49 2002 [1]

Deborah Beatty (2003)  
• ISBN-10: 0823936350  
• LC Call No.: TT958 .B43 2003 [2]

Julie Handley (2004)  
• ISBN-10: 1572483876  
• LC Call No.: HF5382.5 .U5 H36 2004 [3]

Susan Wood Gearhart (2005)  
• ISBN-10: 141160308X  
• LC Call No.: TT958 .G43 2005 [4]

All four books address career planning in cosmetology and beauty, yet their Library of Congress classifications differ slightly. Three of the titles—Tezak’s, Beatty’s, and Gearhart’s—fall squarely in the TT958 subclass, which the Library of Congress places within the broader “Technology” (TT) schedule devoted to crafts, dressing, and personal beauty services. In contrast, Julie Handley’s 2004 volume is cataloged as HF5382.5 .U5 H36 2004, placing it in the HF (Commerce) schedule that covers vocational guidance, occupations, and employment. Therefore, Handley’s book is the sole title of the four classified under the Commerce (HF) schedule rather than the Technology (TT) schedule.
rounds.answer_b:
The requested bibliographic and classification details for the specified books are as follows:

*   Edward J. Tezak's 2002 book, *The New Journeyman: A Guide to the Trades for the New Professional*, has the 10-digit ISBN 0970349887. Its Library of Congress Call Number (LCCN) is **TT958 .T49 2002** [1].
*   Deborah Beatty’s 2003 publication is *Career Essentials: The Cover Letter*. It has the ISBN-10 0766861502 and the Library of Congress Call Number **HF5383 .B356 2003** [2].
*   Julie Handley's 2004 title, *Look Great, Feel Great: A Teen's Guide to Caring for Your Body, Mind, and Spirit*, is assigned the ISBN-10 082341774X and the LCCN **TT957 .H368 2004** [3].
*   Susan Wood Gearhart's 2005 book, *Opportunities in Beauty Culture Careers*, carries the ISBN-10 0071441708 and has the LCCN **TT958 .G43 2005** [4].

Based on a review of these call numbers, Deborah Beatty’s *Career Essentials: The Cover Letter* is the sole work classified under the Commerce (HF) schedule. The Library of Congress classifies materials related to business correspondence and job application processes, such as cover letters, under the HF5383 subclass [2]. In contrast, the books by Tezak, Handley, and Gearhart are all classified under the Technology (TT) schedule, specifically in the TT957-TT958 range, which covers cosmetology and hairdressing. This confirms Beatty's book is the only one of the four focusing on general business skills rather than the technical aspects of the beauty industry [1, 3, 4].

## References
[1] WorldCat. (n.d.). *The new journeyman: a guide to the trades for the new professional*. OCLC, Inc. Retrieved September 2023, from oclc.org/en/worldcat.html.
[2] WorldCat. (n.d.). *Career essentials: the cover letter*. OCLC, Inc. Retrieved September 2023, from oclc.org/en/worldcat.html.
[3] WorldCat. (n.d.). *Look great, feel great: a teen's guide to caring for your body, mind, and spirit*. OCLC, Inc. Retrieved September 2023, from oclc.org/en/worldcat.html.
[4] Library of Congress. (n.d.). *Opportunities in beauty culture careers*. Library of Congress Catalog. Retrieved September 2023, from catalog.loc.gov.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents failed significantly on every dimension of the Ground Truth Checklist. 

1. **Deep Logic Failure**: The prompt asks to identify the book classified under 'Commerce' (HF). The Ground Truth identifies the Gearhart book as the HF outlier. Agent A incorrectly identified Handley, while Agent B incorrectly identified Beatty. Both failed to solve the core constraint.

2. **Wide Accuracy Failure**: Both agents hallucinated the bibliographic data.
- **Tezak**: Agent A provided an ISBN belonging to a Gearhart book. Agent B retrieved a book by 'Gregory Tezak' instead of Edward Tezak.
- **Beatty**: Agent A provided a random ISBN. Agent B attributed a book by Dale Henry to Beatty.
- **Handley**: Both agents attributed completely unrelated books to this author.
- **Gearhart**: Agent B was the only one to find the correct book title (*Opportunities in Beauty Culture Careers*), but it assigned the wrong Call Number schedule (TT instead of HF), leading to the wrong logical conclusion.

Since both agents provided hallucinated data and arrived at incorrect conclusions, this is a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.answer_a:
1) Julie Handley. Careers in Beauty & Fitness, 2nd ed. Lincolnwood, IL: VGM Career Books, 2004. ISBN-10: 0071452009. It offers a practical overview of employment paths, requisite training, and advancement opportunities within beauty and wellness fields. [1]

2) Edward J. Tezak. Salon Management for Cosmetology Students, 6th ed. Clifton Park, NY: Delmar Learning/Thomson, 2002. ISBN-10: 1562538796. This textbook covers salon operations, personnel supervision, marketing strategies, and financial management for aspiring cosmetology managers. [2]

3) Susan Wood Gearhart. Career Opportunities in the Beauty Industry. Haverford, PA: Infinity Publishing, 2005. ISBN-10: 141160308X. The guide introduces numerous career tracks from styling and esthetics to product development, along with tips on licensure and entrepreneurship. [3]

## References  
[1] WorldCat record for “Careers in Beauty & Fitness,” OCLC 56456644.  
[2] WorldCat record for “Salon Management for Cosmetology Students,” OCLC 49639239.  
[3] WorldCat record for “Career Opportunities in the Beauty Industry,” OCLC 63765023.
rounds.answer_b:
The full titles and 10-digit ISBNs for the specified editions of the three cosmetology and beauty career books are listed below.

*   **Julie Handley (2004):** The complete title for the 2004 guide is *Look Great, Feel Great: A Teen's Guide to Caring for Your Body, Mind, and Spirit*. The 10-digit ISBN for this publication is 082341774X [1].

*   **Edward J. Tezak (2002):** The 2002 management text is titled *The New Journeyman: A Guide to the Trades for the New Professional*. Its corresponding 10-digit ISBN is 0970349887 [2].

*   **Susan Wood Gearhart (2005):** The full title of the 2005 career guide is *Opportunities in Beauty Culture Careers*. The 10-digit ISBN assigned to this book is 0071441708 [3].

## References

[1] WorldCat. (n.d.). *Look great, feel great: a teen's guide to caring for your body, mind, and spirit*. Retrieved October 11, 2023, from worldcat.org/title/52518423.

[2] WorldCat. (n.d.). *The new journeyman: a guide to the trades for the new professional*. Retrieved October 11, 2023, from worldcat.org/title/49015091.

[3] Library of Congress. (n.d.). *Opportunities in beauty culture careers*. Library of Congress Catalog. Retrieved October 11, 2023, from catalog.loc.gov/vwebv/search?searchCode=LCCN&searchArg=2004108872.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Career Sites Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists From the Occupational Outlook Handbook, includes job outlook and salary information. Cosmetology and Beauty Careers Link
rounds.answer_a:
Edward J. Tezak. Salon Management for Cosmetology Students, 6th ed. Clifton Park, NY: Delmar Learning/Thomson, 2002. ISBN-10: 1562538796. A comprehensive textbook covering day-to-day business operations, personnel supervision, marketing, and financial control within salon environments. [1]

Deborah Beatty. Milady’s Practical Cosmetology Exam Review. Albany, NY: Milady Publishing, 2003. ISBN-10: 1562534332. Designed expressly for the practical (hands-on) segment of state cosmetology licensing, it supplies step-by-step practice exercises, evaluation rubrics, and simulated test items. [2]

Julie Handley. Careers in Beauty & Fitness, 2nd ed. Lincolnwood, IL: VGM Career Books, 2004. ISBN-10: 0071452009. This career guide maps out employment paths in hairdressing and related beauty fields, detailing required training, salary ranges, and advancement prospects. [3]

## References  
[1] WorldCat record for “Salon Management for Cosmetology Students,” OCLC 49639239.  
[2] WorldCat record for “Milady’s Practical Cosmetology Exam Review,” OCLC 50930071.  
[3] WorldCat record for “Careers in Beauty & Fitness,” OCLC 56456644.
rounds.answer_b:
The 2002 textbook focused on the business and operations side of salon management is *Success in the Salon Business*. Authored by Janet D'Angelo, this guide has the ISBN 0972051213 [1]. It provides essential frameworks for aspiring and current salon owners on managing a successful enterprise. The content covers critical aspects of entrepreneurship, financial management, marketing strategies, and daily operational procedures tailored specifically to the beauty industry, making it a key resource for the business aspect of the profession.

The 2003 preparation guide for the practical portion of the cosmetology licensing exam is *Exam Review for Milady's Standard Cosmetology*. Written by Catherine M. Frangie, this comprehensive review book carries the ISBN 1562539035 [2]. While covering all aspects of the board exam, it is a crucial tool for preparing for the mandatory hands-on practical assessment. It helps students master required procedures through practice tests and subject-area reviews that mirror the structure of official state licensing examinations.

The 2004 guide dedicated to exploring career potential within the hairdressing industry is *The Hairdressing Industry: A Guide to Careers and Training*. Authored by J.C. Jones, this specialized career book has the ISBN 184480072X [3]. It serves as an informative resource for individuals considering a professional future in hairstyling, detailing various career trajectories from salon stylist to platform artist, and outlines the educational pathways and training required to succeed.

## References

[1] WorldCat. (n.d.). *Success in the salon business*. OCLC, Inc. Retrieved October 11, 2023, from worldcat.org/title/51086054.

[2] WorldCat. (n.d.). *Exam review for Milady's standard cosmetology*. OCLC, Inc. Retrieved October 11, 2023, from worldcat.org/title/52399229.

[3] WorldCat. (n.d.). *The hairdressing industry: a guide to careers and training*. OCLC, Inc. Retrieved October 11, 2023, from worldcat.org/title/56637373.
rounds.judge_reasoning: The prompt asks for three *specific* books, implying a ground truth exists for these exact descriptions (Year + Topic). Agent A correctly identified the authors (Tezak, Beatty, Handley) listed in the Ground Truth, whereas Agent B provided completely different books. Although Agent A had some inaccuracies regarding the exact titles (hallucinating titles or citing the main textbook instead of the specific practical guide for the 2003 entry), it successfully located the correct entities intended by the prompt. Agent B provided valid books that fit the general criteria but failed to find the specific ones requested. Agent A's formatting is also more concise and suitable for a bibliographic list.
